medical_history.history_of_present_illness: 1 year old male who originally presented to after injecting heroin into his neck this AM at a motel. A CT of the neck showed severe parotitis, though there were concerns for necrotizing soft tissue infection. He was started on vancomycin/Zosyn/clindamycin and transferred for surgical evaluation. Initial vitals were stable and he did not require pressors. Repeat CT showed interval progression of inflammatory changes concerning for worsening infection.

physical_examination.general_examination: Intubated, sedated and responding to vocal stimulus. Initial vitals: HR 115, BP 115/69, Temp 32. CT Neck as above.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Significant edema of R hand, 7x7cm erythematous, denuded skin on R shoulder with well demarcated border. 4x5 cm flaccid bullae on R wrist dorsum. Yellow serous exudate on neck with significant subcutaneous edema. Opens eyes to voice, follows 1 step commands, PERRL, +corneal, +gag.
